h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's step-by-step instructions on the worksheet without encountering a conceptual conflict.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets three blank tiles up to the bar line, puts the eighth-note card in the lower box, and draws an eighth note on each tile]

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place cards and identify forces on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Track on cart. For the support contact, the track pushes up on the cart, so this arrow should point up when the strip lifts. If this card is also supposed to cover rubbing, that needs a separate sideways arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the force-identification rule to the track, predicts the arrow direction, and identifies the need for a separate arrow for friction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [places a blank strip over the arrowheads, leaving only the card spaces visible] The card named the first force. The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart. Put track in the slot. Say: track on cart. Then lift the strip and choose the direction for that named interaction.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's struggle with the arrowhead's visual cue by physically covering the arrowheads with a blank strip, changing the task's representation and sequence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.
